description: Real audit benchmark cases using public OSS RAG/vector database docs, public GitHub issue snippets, and ContextTrace documentation snippets. Cases are embedded so benchmark runs are local and deterministic.

cases.note: Supporting Qdrant payload evidence exists in corpus but retrieved context is from filtering docs.
cases.query: How does Qdrant store payloads?
cases.answer: Qdrant stores additional information with vectors as payload.

retrieved_contexts.text: Filtering lets you define conditions that must match payload values during search.

corpus_contexts.text: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

cases.note: Chroma metadata filter evidence exists in corpus but was not retrieved.
cases.query: How does Chroma filter documents by metadata?
cases.answer: Chroma supports filtering results with a where metadata clause.

retrieved_contexts.text: A Chroma collection stores embeddings, documents, and metadata together.

corpus_contexts.text: Chroma supports filtering results with a where metadata clause when querying a collection.

cases.note: Haystack Document Store support exists in corpus but retriever returned pipeline text.
cases.query: What does a Haystack Document Store store?
cases.answer: A Haystack Document Store stores documents for retrieval.

retrieved_contexts.text: Haystack pipelines connect components that process inputs and produce outputs.

corpus_contexts.text: A Document Store stores documents and makes them available to Retrievers.

cases.note: LangChain retriever source exists in corpus but only tracing docs were retrieved.
cases.query: What does VectorStoreRetriever do?
cases.answer: VectorStoreRetriever retrieves documents from a vector store.

retrieved_contexts.text: Callbacks let LangChain users observe chain and model execution events.

corpus_contexts.text: VectorStoreRetriever retrieves documents from a vector store by calling the configured search.

cases.note: ContextTrace local DB evidence exists in corpus but report docs were retrieved.
cases.query: Where does ContextTrace store traces in local mode?
cases.answer: ContextTrace stores local traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.

retrieved_contexts.text: ContextTrace writes local HTML reports under .contexttrace/reports/.

corpus_contexts.text: Local mode is the default. ContextTrace stores local traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.

cases.note: Same Qdrant source was retrieved, but the chunk omitted the array filtering span.
cases.query: How does Qdrant filter array payloads?
cases.answer: When Qdrant filters an array payload, the filter succeeds if at least one value inside the array meets the condition.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

corpus_contexts.text: Arrays are treated differently during filtering. When a filter is applied to an array, it succeeds if at least one of the values inside the array meets the condition.

cases.note: Filtering source was retrieved without the payload index recommendation span.
cases.query: What does Qdrant recommend for performant filtering?
cases.answer: For performant filtering, Qdrant recommends creating payload indexes for fields you plan to filter on.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant filtering lets you restrict search results by payload conditions.

corpus_contexts.text: For performant filtering, create payload indexes for the fields you plan to filter on.

cases.note: GitHub issue source was retrieved but not the no-result where-filter span.
cases.query: What can happen when a Chroma where filter has no matches?
cases.answer: A Chroma where filter can return no matching results.

retrieved_contexts.text: The Chroma issue discusses query behavior with where filters.

corpus_contexts.text: A query using a where filter can return no results when no metadata values match the condition.

cases.note: Privacy source was retrieved but omitted the no-network span.
cases.query: Does ContextTrace require network calls in local mode?
cases.answer: ContextTrace does not make network calls unless a judge provider or endpoint is configured.

retrieved_contexts.text: Local mode stores traces on the developer machine.

corpus_contexts.text: ContextTrace does not make network calls unless you configure a judge provider or point it at an endpoint you control. Ollama and local OpenAI-compatible judge providers call localhost by default.

cases.note: Correct source appears at rank 4, below the audit reranking cutoff.
cases.query: What does Qdrant match any do?
cases.answer: Qdrant match any checks whether a stored value is one of several supplied values.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant supports vector search.
Payloads store extra information.
Indexes can improve filtering.
Filtering conditions can be combined with must and should clauses.

corpus_contexts.text: Match Any checks if the stored value is one of multiple supplied values.

cases.note: Haystack retriever source is present but ranked too low.
cases.query: What do Haystack retrievers do?
cases.answer: Haystack retrievers fetch relevant documents from a Document Store.

retrieved_contexts.text: Haystack components can be connected in pipelines.
Haystack has several component types for pipelines.
Readers can extract answers from documents.
The Haystack retrievers documentation describes available components.

corpus_contexts.text: Retrievers fetch relevant documents from a Document Store for a user query.

cases.note: LangChain retriever evidence is in rank 4.
cases.query: How does VectorStoreRetriever get documents?
cases.answer: VectorStoreRetriever gets documents by calling a configured vector store search.

retrieved_contexts.text: LangChain callbacks observe execution.
Runnable objects can be composed.
Vector stores contain embeddings.
VectorStoreRetriever is a retriever class.

corpus_contexts.text: VectorStoreRetriever retrieves documents from a vector store by calling the configured search.

cases.note: Corpus explicitly conflicts with the hosted dashboard requirement.
cases.query: Does ContextTrace require a hosted dashboard?
cases.answer: ContextTrace requires a hosted dashboard to inspect traces.

retrieved_contexts.text: ContextTrace is a local-first SDK and CLI.

corpus_contexts.text: ContextTrace is local-first and does not require a hosted dashboard.

cases.note: Answer invents pricing details not present in the supplied public docs snippet.
cases.query: How much does a Qdrant payload index cost?
cases.answer: A Qdrant payload index costs 10 dollars per collection.

retrieved_contexts.text: Payload indexes can improve filtering performance.

corpus_contexts.text: Payload indexes can improve filtering performance for fields used in filters.

cases.note: Corpus supports where filters, but the answer adds unsupported SQL join behavior.
cases.query: Can Chroma where filters use SQL joins?
cases.answer: Chroma where filters support SQL joins across collections.

retrieved_contexts.text: Chroma supports metadata filtering with where clauses.

corpus_contexts.text: Chroma supports metadata filtering with where clauses on collection queries.

cases.note: Corpus supports payload storage, but answer adds encryption claim.
cases.query: What does Qdrant store with vectors?
cases.answer: Qdrant stores payload information with vectors and encrypts every payload by default.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

corpus_contexts.text: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

cases.note: Corpus supports document storage, not automatic duplicate removal.
cases.query: What does a Haystack Document Store do?
cases.answer: A Haystack Document Store stores documents and automatically removes duplicate content.

retrieved_contexts.text: A Document Store stores documents and makes them available to Retrievers.

corpus_contexts.text: A Document Store stores documents and makes them available to Retrievers.

cases.note: Corpus supports local DB storage, not automatic cloud sync.
cases.query: Where does ContextTrace store traces?
cases.answer: ContextTrace stores traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db and automatically syncs them to the cloud.

retrieved_contexts.text: ContextTrace stores traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.

corpus_contexts.text: ContextTrace stores traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.

cases.note: Viewer port conflicts with current docs.
cases.query: Where does the ContextTrace viewer run?
cases.answer: The ContextTrace viewer runs at http://localhost:9999.

retrieved_contexts.text: The local viewer serves pages at http://localhost:8765.

corpus_contexts.text: The local viewer reads from .contexttrace/contexttrace.db and serves pages at http://localhost:8765.

cases.note: Python version claim conflicts with current requirement.
cases.query: What Python version does ContextTrace require?
cases.answer: ContextTrace supports Python 3.7 and later.

retrieved_contexts.text: ContextTrace requires Python 3.8+.

corpus_contexts.text: ContextTrace requires Python 3.8+.

cases.note: Corpus supports payload filtering, but not the added ranking interaction.
cases.query: How do Qdrant filters affect ranking?
cases.answer: Qdrant filters combine payload conditions with vector ranking.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant filtering applies conditions during search.

corpus_contexts.text: Qdrant filtering applies payload conditions during search.

cases.note: Corpus does not support the deterministic ranking guarantee.
cases.query: Does VectorStoreRetriever guarantee deterministic ranking?
cases.answer: VectorStoreRetriever guarantees deterministic ranking for every query.

retrieved_contexts.text: VectorStoreRetriever retrieves documents from a vector store by calling the configured search.

corpus_contexts.text: VectorStoreRetriever retrieves documents from a vector store by calling the configured search.

cases.note: Corpus is related to filtering, but too weak to verify a distance-scoring claim.
cases.query: Do Qdrant filters change vector distance scoring?
cases.answer: Qdrant filtering changes vector distance scoring.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant supports vector search over collections.

corpus_contexts.text: Qdrant filtering applies conditions during search.

cases.note: Corpus explains retriever purpose, but not ranking explanations.
cases.query: Do Haystack retrievers explain document ranking?
cases.answer: Haystack retrievers explain why each document was ranked.

retrieved_contexts.text: Haystack pipelines connect components that process inputs and outputs.

corpus_contexts.text: Retrievers fetch relevant documents from a Document Store for a user query.

cases.note: Healthy audit case where retrieved evidence already supports the claim.
cases.query: What is a Qdrant payload?
cases.answer: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

retrieved_contexts.text: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

corpus_contexts.text: Qdrant stores additional information along with vectors as payload.

cases.note: Healthy ContextTrace local storage claim.
cases.query: Where does ContextTrace store local traces?
cases.answer: ContextTrace stores local traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.

retrieved_contexts.text: ContextTrace stores local traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.

corpus_contexts.text: ContextTrace stores local traces in .contexttrace/contexttrace.db.
